behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is module handoff: changed-file collection -> analysis scheduler. A unit test of only the filter function can miss wiring mistakes (e.g., scheduler still iterates full repo set). Integration is sufficient because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess. Rejected System: no behavior text requiring real GitHub API or git subprocess semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire changed-file provider, file-selection logic, and analyzer dispatcher together with a fixture PR diff and workspace files; assert analyzer receives only changed paths and never unchanged paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when rule outputs, grouping logic, and comment rendering/post payload wiring interact. Unit tests of grouping alone can miss formatting/payload mismatches. Integration-brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess for correctness (API can be stubbed), no AI/ML output dependence, no multi-step agent chain. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on grouping in posted content, not live GitHub transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run analyzer result aggregation + comment formatter + PR-comment client adapter against mixed-severity fixtures; verify generated posted body contains correctly partitioned error/warning/info sections and correct finding membership.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a runtime/timeout behavior in GitHub Actions. Real failure depends on process scheduling, filesystem I/O, and CI runner performance characteristics; in-process mocks can hide timeout regressions. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output decisions involved.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real lint step (same command/path as Action) on a generated 200-file repo workload in CI-like environment and assert end-to-end wall-clock completion under 600 seconds with findings emitted.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly references a linter process crash; crash handling requires real subprocess exit/crash semantics and continuation logic across remaining files. Mocking subprocess often hides signal/exit-code and stream-handling edge cases. Rejected Integration: disqualified by behavior text 'linter process crashes'. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with a real linter executable configured so one malformed file causes process crash/non-zero termination; assert pipeline continues analyzing other files and posts consolidated results including unaffected files.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is interaction of multiple rule outputs with dedup stage and output rendering. A pure unit test on dedup key logic may miss schema/normalization differences between rule modules. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI/ML output dependence; no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two overlapping rule modules through the aggregation pipeline to produce duplicate findings on identical file/line, then verify final output contains one canonical entry per dedup key.
